title: Fix all silent .catch(() => {}) in observatory frontend (10 violations)
description:
Replace all 10 empty .catch(() => {}) handlers in the observatory frontend with console.warn() logging. Each catch should include a descriptive message and the error object.

Violations to fix:
1. hooks/useMetrics.ts:39 — GDP history fetch → console.warn('GDP history fetch failed:', e)
2. hooks/useTaskDrilldown.ts:47 — task polling → console.warn('Task poll failed:', e)
3. hooks/useAgentProfile.ts:52 — profile+earnings load → console.warn('Agent profile load failed:', e)
4. hooks/useAgentProfile.ts:69 — feed filter load → console.warn('Agent feed load failed:', e)
5. hooks/useAgentProfile.ts:86 — feed polling → console.warn('Feed poll failed:', e)
6. hooks/useAgentProfile.ts:104 — feed pagination → console.warn('Feed pagination failed:', e)
7. hooks/useAgents.ts:12 — top workers fetch → console.warn('Top workers fetch failed:', e)
8. hooks/useAgents.ts:15 — top posters fetch → console.warn('Top posters fetch failed:', e)
9. components/landing/LiveProofSection.tsx:129 — metrics fetch → console.warn('Metrics fetch failed:', e)
10. components/landing/LiveProofSection.tsx:132 — events fetch → console.warn('Events fetch failed:', e)

Note: useTaskDrilldown.ts:28 already handles errors properly (setError(true)) — do NOT touch it.

Verify: cd services/observatory && uv run semgrep --config ../../config/semgrep/no-silent-catch.yml --error frontend/src should report 0 findings after fix.
close_reason: Semgrep rules created and all violations fixed in commit d08a8e1

title: E2E test: agent credits account and views transactions
description: Write an e2e test where an agent registers, gets a bank account, receives a credit, and views transaction history. Uses existing IdentityMixin and BankMixin. Test: register → platform creates account → platform credits funds → agent.get_balance() verifies updated balance → agent.get_transactions() verifies credit entry appears.
close_reason: E2E test implemented in agents/tests/e2e/test_bank.py::test_credit_account_and_view_transactions

title: [observatory] Fix startup resilience: fail fast or degrade gracefully when DB missing
description:
## Problem

`core/lifespan.py:33-42` — When the database file is missing at startup, the lifespan catches the exception and logs a warning, but the service starts with `state.db = None`.

Every router then does `assert db is not None` (e.g., `routers/metrics.py:36-37`) which raises an unhandled `AssertionError` → raw 500 error on every request. There is no graceful degradation and no structured error response. The service appears healthy via the process but fails on every endpoint.

## Suggested Fix

Either:
1. **Fail fast**: Refuse to start if the database is unavailable (raise during lifespan startup), OR
2. **Degrade gracefully**: Replace `assert db is not None` with proper checks that return structured 503 responses, and have the health endpoint report `"status": "degraded"` when `db is None`.

Option 1 is simpler and recommended for a read-only service that cannot serve any purpose without its database.

## Principle

Design for Failure / Resilience

## Identified at

Git HEAD: `2c08c347` (2c08c3478a5baa2a126c6e09f52441ddececeb55)

title: E2E test: economic cycle - earn then spend
description: Write an e2e test demonstrating a full economic cycle where an agent earns money then uses it. Requires TaskBoardMixin + BankMixin. Test: register agent A + agent B → fund agent A → A posts task → B bids → A accepts → B submits → A approves → verify B earned reward → B posts a NEW task using earned funds → A bids on B's task → verify full circular fund flow.
close_reason: E2E tests implemented and CI passes

title: Implement feeder auto-approval — review submitted solutions against known answers
description:
Add a review_loop module to agents/src/task_feeder/ that runs concurrently with the feed loop. The review loop must: (1) Poll for tasks posted by feeder in REVIEW status. (2) Fetch submitted deliverables. (3) Look up known solutions from JSONL data (maintain task_id->RawTask mapping). (4) Compare submitted answer against known solutions (case-insensitive, whitespace-trimmed). (5) Approve if match, dispute if not (with expected-vs-received reason). Run as separate asyncio.Task in __main__.py sharing the same BaseAgent instance. Add config fields: review_interval_seconds, auto_approve_on_error (from config.yaml). All existing tests must pass (just ci-quiet from agents/).

Files to read: agents/src/task_feeder/loop.py, agents/src/task_feeder/reader.py, agents/src/task_feeder/config.py, agents/src/task_feeder/__main__.py, agents/src/base_agent/task_board_mixin.py, data/math_tasks.jsonl, agents/config.yaml

title: Decompose TaskManager god class (2108 lines) into focused services
description: The TaskManager class in services/task-board/src/task_board_service/services/task_manager.py is a 2108-line god class with 25+ public methods spanning 6+ concerns: task CRUD, deadline evaluation/transitions, escrow management with retry logic, asset file storage, JWS token validation, database schema initialization, statistics, and file path security. This violates SRP (6 reasons to change), DIP (concrete client dependencies, hardcoded sqlite3.connect), ISP (all consumers get the full class), and OCP (adding new task statuses requires modifying deadline evaluation). Decompose into: TaskLifecycleService, DeadlineEvaluator, EscrowCoordinator, AssetManager, TokenValidator, and TaskRepository. Define protocols (AuthVerifier, EscrowClient, Signer) for external dependencies. Extract SQLite access behind a TaskRepository protocol. Also fix duplicated _parse_json_body/_extract_token helpers across routers/tasks.py, routers/bids.py, routers/assets.py, the hardcoded endpoint patterns in middleware.py, and the __setattr__ magic in AppState. Use the Reputation service as the reference for clean layering.
close_reason: TaskManager decomposed from 2108 to 1265 lines. Extracted 4 focused services (EscrowCoordinator, TokenValidator, DeadlineEvaluator, AssetManager) + shared router validation helpers. Removed AppState __setattr__ magic. All 347 tests pass, full CI green. 7 commits, one per task.

title: Write acceptance tests for: fund-feeder script
description: Write failing tests that verify the fund-feeder script (tools/fund-feeder.sh): (1) accepts funding amount as CLI argument with no hardcoded default, (2) uses Ed25519 signing for authenticated requests, (3) registers the feeder agent if not already registered, (4) transfers funds via the platform agent using the central-bank salary endpoint, (5) prints a summary with agent_id, funded amount, and resulting balance, (6) exits 0 on success and 1 on failure. Tests can be shell-based or pytest-based integration tests.

(2) Confirm whether services/observatory/config.yaml database.path resolves to the same absolute path tools/seed-economy.sh writes to. (3) If paths differ, fix the config or seed script. (4) Add a startup check in observatory lifespan: if the SQLite file does not exist, log a clear error naming the expected path and exit non-zero instead of silently failing. (5) All existing observatory tests must continue to pass (just ci-quiet from services/observatory/).

Files to read: services/observatory/config.yaml, services/observatory/src/observatory_service/config.py, services/observatory/src/observatory_service/core/lifespan.py, tools/seed-economy.sh, docs/specifications/schema.sql

title: Add scenario test infrastructure and first identity scenario test
description: Create agents/tests/scenarios/ directory with conftest.py and test_identity.py. The scenario test starts a BaseAgent, registers it against a live Identity service, then exercises get_agent_info, list_agents, and verify_signature. Requires the identity service running on localhost:8001. Add pytest mark @pytest.mark.scenario. Add just test-scenarios target to agents/justfile (runs pytest tests/scenarios/ -v -m scenario). Add just test-scenarios target to root justfile (delegates to cd agents && just test-scenarios). Scenario tests are NOT part of ci-quiet since they need live services.

title: [observatory] Fix compute_gdp_history N+1 query explosion (10k queries per request)
description:
## Problem

`services/metrics.py:543-571` — `compute_gdp_history()` issues one `compute_gdp_at_timestamp()` SQL query per time step. A request for `window=7d&resolution=1m` triggers **10,080 sequential SQL queries**, each scanning the full `board_tasks` table.

There is no timeout, no pagination limit, no circuit breaker. Combined with the single shared `aiosqlite.Connection`, one API call blocks all other concurrent requests for seconds or minutes.

## Suggested Fix

Replace the Python loop with a single window-based SQL query using `GROUP BY` on time buckets (e.g., `strftime` in SQLite), or pre-aggregate GDP at fixed intervals. At minimum, cap the max number of data points returned and add a request timeout.

## Principle

Design for Failure / Resilience

## Identified at

Git HEAD: `2c08c347` (2c08c3478a5baa2a126c6e09f52441ddececeb55)

title: Decompose DisputeService god class (653 lines) into focused services
description: The DisputeService class in services/court/src/court_service/services/dispute_service.py is a 653-line class with 20+ methods mixing persistence (SQLite schema init, raw SQL), dispute lifecycle operations, judge evaluation orchestration, escrow splitting, feedback recording, task ruling, and vote normalization. This violates SRP (multiple reasons to change) and DIP (hardcoded sqlite3.connect in constructor). Additionally, the disputes.py router (~340 lines) has validation helpers (_parse_json_body, _extract_jws_token, _verify_jws) that call get_app_state() internally (service locator anti-pattern), making them hard to test or reuse. Decompose into: DisputeRepository (persistence), DisputeRulingOrchestrator (judge evaluation + side effects), and DisputeValidator (preconditions/state transitions). Extract SQLite access behind a DisputeRepository protocol. Move router validation helpers to accept dependencies as parameters or use FastAPI Depends().
close_reason: DisputeService decomposed: RulingOrchestrator extracted (214 lines, 12 methods), router validation helpers extracted (6 functions). All 99+12+14 tests pass. CI green.

title: Fix log filename: use YYYY-MM-DD.log instead of current.log
description: The active log file is named current.log and only gets renamed to YYYY-MM-DD.log on midnight rotation. The design intent was for log files to always be named YYYY-MM-DD.log. Fix setup_logging() in service-commons to use today's date as the filename directly, so the active log is e.g. 2026-03-01.log from the start.
close_reason: Already fixed - DailyRotatingFileHandler in service-commons names files YYYY-MM-DD.log from creation.

Also test: single feedback becomes visible after reveal_timeout_seconds.

(3) Uses the platform agent to transfer initial balance via central-bank salary endpoint. (4) Verifies feeder balance is sufficient. Script must use curl/httpx with Ed25519 signing (reuse data/keys/platform.key and data/keys/feeder.key, follow tools/seed-economy.sh signing pattern). Must accept funding amount as CLI argument (no hardcoded default). Must print summary (agent_id, funded amount, balance) and exit 0/1. Add justfile target fund-feeder at project root with sensible default amount. Document in help section.

title: E2E Test Gap Coverage
description: Epic covering 16 new e2e test scenarios identified by gap analysis against system sequence diagrams. Five new test files: test_court_rulings.py, test_reputation_sealed.py, test_asset_store.py, test_platform_auth.py, test_economic_invariants.py. See docs/plans/2026-03-01-e2e-test-gap-coverage-design.md for full design.

title: E2E test: escrow split proportional payout after court ruling
description:
Confirm that after a court ruling, escrow is split proportionally — not just that the sum is correct.

Setup: poster (5000 tokens), worker (0). Post task with reward=1000. Drive full lifecycle to disputed state. File dispute via platform, submit rebuttal, trigger ruling.

Assertions:
- worker_balance == worker_pct * 1000 / 100
- poster_balance == 5000 - 1000 + (100 - worker_pct) * 1000 / 100
- Both values individually correct, not just sum == 5000

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard, Court.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_court_rulings.py

This test may fail if Court service does not call POST /escrow/{id}/split on Central Bank after ruling.

title: Wire Court escrow split side-effect end-to-end
description:
Ensure the Court service calls POST /escrow/{escrow_id}/split on Central Bank after a ruling is made. The split should use the median worker_pct from judge votes.

The Court spec (docs/specifications/service-api/court-service-specs.md) defines the ruling side-effects order:
1. POST /escrow/{escrow_id}/split on Central Bank
2. POST /feedback x2 on Reputation
3. POST /tasks/{task_id}/ruling on TaskBoard

Check services/court/src/court_service/services/ for the dispute resolution logic and verify step 1 is implemented.

title: E2E test: multiple asset uploads per task
description:
Confirm that multiple files can be uploaded to a single task and all are retrievable.

Setup: poster (5000), worker (0). Post task, bid, accept.

Steps and assertions:
1. Worker uploads 3 files: 'code.py' (b'print(1)'), 'readme.md' (b'# Hello'), 'data.json' (b'{"key": 1}')
2. List assets via GET /tasks/{task_id}/assets
3. Assert: 3 assets returned
4. Assert: filenames match {'code.py', 'readme.md', 'data.json'}
5. Assert: each asset has a non-empty asset_id and size_bytes > 0

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_asset_store.py

Check services/court/src/court_service/services/ for the dispute resolution logic and verify step 3 (POST /tasks/{task_id}/ruling) is implemented and the TaskBoard endpoint handles it correctly.

title: E2E test: submit deliverable without assets rejected
description:
Adversarial: verify that a worker cannot submit a deliverable when no assets have been uploaded.

Setup: poster (5000), worker (0). Post task, bid, accept. Do NOT upload any assets.

Steps and assertions:
1. Worker calls submit_deliverable(task_id)
2. Assert: error response (the TaskBoard spec says submit requires at least 1 asset)
3. Assert: task status remains 'accepted' (not transitioned to 'submitted')

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_asset_store.py

This test may fail if the 'at least 1 asset' validation is not enforced in the TaskBoard implementation.

title: Wire Court reputation feedback posting end-to-end
description:
Ensure the Court service posts two feedback records to Reputation after a ruling:
- spec_quality feedback targeting the poster
- delivery_quality feedback targeting the worker

Check services/court/src/court_service/services/ for the dispute resolution logic and verify step 2 (POST /feedback x2) is implemented.

title: E2E test: non-platform agent cannot release escrow
description:
Adversarial: verify that a regular (non-platform) agent cannot release escrow by signing their own JWS.

Setup: poster (5000), worker (0). Post task (escrow is now locked). Get the escrow_id from the task.

Steps and assertions:
1. Regular worker agent signs a JWS with payload { action: 'escrow_release', escrow_id: <id>, recipient_account_id: worker.agent_id }
2. Send POST /escrow/{escrow_id}/release to Central Bank with { token: <signed_jws> }
3. Assert: 403 FORBIDDEN (Central Bank should reject non-platform signatures for escrow operations)
4. Assert: poster balance unchanged (escrow still locked)

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_platform_auth.py

Uses worker._sign_jws() and worker._request_raw() for raw HTTP access.

title: Enforce minimum 1 asset on deliverable submission
description:
The TaskBoard API spec requires at least 1 uploaded asset before a worker can submit a deliverable. Verify this validation exists in services/task-board/src/task_board_service/services/ and add it if missing.

When no assets exist for the task, POST /tasks/{task_id}/submit should return an error (400 or 409) with a clear error code like NO_ASSETS or similar.

Check: services/task-board/src/task_board_service/services/tasks.py for the submit_deliverable logic.

title: E2E test: non-platform agent cannot split escrow

Adversarial: verify that a regular (non-platform) agent cannot split escrow.

Setup: poster (5000), worker (0). Post task, bid, accept, upload, submit, dispute. Get the escrow_id.

Steps and assertions:
1. Worker signs JWS with payload { action: 'escrow_split', escrow_id: <id>, worker_account_id: worker.agent_id, poster_account_id: poster.agent_id, worker_pct: 100 }
2. Send POST /escrow/{escrow_id}/split to Central Bank with { token: <signed_jws> }
3. Assert: 403 FORBIDDEN
4. Assert: balances unchanged

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_platform_auth.py

title: E2E test: non-platform agent cannot credit another account

Adversarial: verify that a regular agent cannot credit funds to any account.

Setup: two registered agents, A (balance 100) and B (balance 0).

Steps and assertions:
1. Agent A signs JWS with payload { action: 'credit', account_id: B.agent_id, amount: 500, reference: 'fake_salary' }
2. Send POST to Central Bank credit endpoint with { token: <signed_jws> }
3. Assert: 403 FORBIDDEN
4. Assert: B's balance remains 0, A's balance remains 100

Services: Identity, Bank.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_platform_auth.py

title: E2E test: economic cycle with dispute and partial payout
description:
Confirm that partial payouts from court disputes flow correctly into subsequent economic activity.

Setup: Agent A (5000 tokens), Agent B (0 tokens).

Round 1 — Dispute:
1. A posts task (reward=1000), B bids, A accepts, B uploads asset, B submits deliverable
2. A disputes. Platform files dispute with Court. Worker submits rebuttal. Trigger ruling.
3. Assume ruling worker_pct=N. Assert: B gets N*1000/100, A gets 5000-1000+(100-N)*1000/100

Round 2 — B spends earnings:
4. B posts a new task using partial earnings (reward=200, must be <= B's balance)
5. A bids, B accepts, A uploads, A submits, B approves
6. Assert: A's final balance = previous + 200, B's final balance = previous - 200
7. Assert: total money in system == 5000 (conservation invariant)

Services: All five (Identity, Bank, TaskBoard, Court, Reputation).
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_economic_invariants.py

This test depends on the full Court dispute pipeline working end-to-end. If Court side-effects are not wired, this test will fail.

title: E2E test: insufficient funds cannot post task
description:
Adversarial: verify that an agent with insufficient balance cannot post a task.

Setup: agent with balance 100. Attempts to post task with reward=500.

Steps and assertions:

2. Assert: error response (escrow lock should fail with 402 INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS or similar)
3. Assert: no task is created (list_tasks does not contain the task)
4. Assert: agent balance remains 100

This differs from test_escrow_lock_insufficient_funds in test_bank.py which tests the raw escrow endpoint. This test verifies the full task posting flow handles the failure gracefully.

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard.

title: E2E test: court posts reputation feedback after ruling

Confirm that after a court ruling, the Court service posts feedback to the Reputation service for both parties.

- One record has category='delivery_quality' targeting the worker (to_agent_id == worker.agent_id)

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard, Court, Reputation.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_court_rulings.py

This test may fail if Court service does not call POST /feedback on Reputation after ruling.

title: E2E test: sealed feedback invisible until mutual submission
description:
Confirm the sealed feedback mechanism works end-to-end: one-sided feedback is hidden until both parties submit.

Setup: full happy path — poster (5000), worker (0). Post task, bid, accept, upload, submit, approve.

Steps and assertions:
1. Poster submits feedback for worker (category='delivery_quality', rating='satisfied')
2. GET /feedback/task/{task_id} — assert empty list (feedback is sealed, only one side submitted)
3. GET /feedback/{feedback_id} using the ID from step 1 — assert 404 (sealed feedback indistinguishable from non-existent)
4. Worker submits feedback for poster (category='spec_quality', rating='satisfied')
5. GET /feedback/task/{task_id} — assert 2 records, both visible
6. GET /feedback/{feedback_id} from step 1 — assert 200, visible=true

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard, Reputation.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_reputation_sealed.py

This test should pass against current code — the sealed mechanism is implemented in the Reputation service.

title: E2E test: self-feedback rejected

Setup: poster (5000), worker (0). Full happy path through approval.

Steps and assertions:

2. Assert: 400 SELF_FEEDBACK error returned
3. GET /feedback/task/{task_id} — assert no self-feedback records exist

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard, Reputation.

title: E2E test: dispute proceeds without worker rebuttal
description:
Edge case: verify that a court ruling can proceed even if the worker never submits a rebuttal.

Setup: poster (5000), worker (0). Full lifecycle to disputed state. File dispute via platform. Do NOT submit rebuttal. Trigger ruling immediately.

Assertions:
- Ruling either succeeds (status 200, dispute status becomes 'ruled') OR
- Fails with a specific documented error (INVALID_DISPUTE_STATUS if rebuttal is required)
- If ruling succeeds: escrow is distributed, task status updates

The Court spec says rebuttal is optional (rebuttal field is nullable). This test confirms that behavior.

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard, Court.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_court_rulings.py

title: E2E test: duplicate feedback rejected
description:
Adversarial: verify that the same (task_id, from_agent_id, to_agent_id) feedback pair cannot be submitted twice.

Setup: full happy path through approval.

Steps and assertions:
1. Poster submits feedback for worker (category='delivery_quality', rating='satisfied', comment='Good'). Assert 201.
2. Poster submits feedback for worker again (same task, same agents, any rating/comment). Assert 409 FEEDBACK_EXISTS.
3. Worker submits feedback for poster. Assert 201 (different direction is allowed).

Services: Identity, Bank, TaskBoard, Reputation.
File: agents/tests/e2e/test_reputation_sealed.py

This test should pass against current code — uniqueness constraint exists.

Assertions:

- Original dispute is unaffected

title: E2E test: competing bidders
description: Write an e2e test with multiple workers bidding on the same task. Requires TaskBoardMixin. Test: register poster + 3 workers → fund poster → post task → all 3 workers bid with different amounts → poster accepts one bid → verify task assigned to correct worker → verify all bids still visible after acceptance → verify non-selected workers can still bid on other tasks.
close_reason: E2E tests implemented and CI passes

title: Investigate and document who populates the events table
description:
Design investigation — no code changes required. Produce a concise markdown document at docs/plans/events-architecture.md (under 200 lines) that answers: (1) Which services need to emit events, and which event types each service owns (reference event_source and event_type columns). (2) Where in each service the event emission should be added (specific files in services/<name>/src/<name>_service/services/). (3) Whether events should be written directly to SQLite or via a central event collector. (4) Recommendation with justification.

Files to read: docs/specifications/schema.sql (events table), tools/seed-economy.sh (current emit pattern), services/observatory/src/observatory_service/services/events.py (how events are read), services/task-board/src/task_board_service/services/ (representative mutations)

title: E2E test: sealed bid visibility rules

Test: register poster + worker + observer → post task → worker bids → poster can list bids (auth required, sealed phase) → observer cannot list bids (no auth, 403/401) → poster accepts bid → observer CAN now list bids (public after OPEN phase ends).
close_reason: E2E tests implemented and CI passes

title: E2E test: full dispute with court ruling and escrow split

Test: register poster + worker → fund poster → post task → bid → accept → submit → poster disputes → worker submits rebuttal → court rules (proportional split, e.g., 60/40) → verify task status RULED → verify escrow split between poster and worker per ruling → verify both agents' balances updated correctly.
close_reason: E2E tests implemented and CI passes

title: E2E test: accept a bid and assign worker

title: E2E test: post a task with escrow

title: Pre-existing: Task Board escrow release fails with 400 from Central Bank
description: The Task Board fails to release escrow on cancel/approve/deadline operations. Logs show: 'Central Bank unexpected status on escrow release, status_code: 400'. Root cause appears to be empty platform_agent_id in task-board config (platform.agent_id is empty string). This means platform-signed JWS tokens for escrow release have no valid kid, so the Central Bank rejects them. Affects: test_post_and_cancel_task, test_happy_path_submit_and_approve, test_execution_deadline_expiry, test_auto_approve_on_review_timeout, test_economic_cycle_earn_then_spend, test_mutual_feedback_exchange.

Tests must be syntactically valid and CI-compliant but expected to fail against current code.

close_reason: E2E test implemented in agents/tests/e2e/test_bank.py::test_escrow_lock_insufficient_funds

title: Agent startup missing bank account provisioning
description: Both task_feeder/__main__.py and math_worker/__main__.py call agent.register() but never call platform.create_account() to provision a central bank account. Account creation is a platform-only operation — regular agents can't create their own accounts. Without a bank account, agents fail at their first economic operation (posting a task, checking balance, etc.). The e2e test test_agent_startup.py demonstrates this: a registered agent gets 502/404 errors when trying to post tasks or check balance. Fix requires adding a platform agent step to the agent startup flow that provisions a bank account after registration.
close_reason: Implemented self-service account creation: agents can now create their own zero-balance bank accounts after registration. Central bank POST /accounts accepts both platform and agent JWS tokens. Agent startup (feeder/mathbot) now calls create_account() after register().

All methods decorated with @tool for Strands compatibility. All async using httpx.

title: Fix broad except Exception in observatory lifespan.py
description:
Narrow the except Exception: block in services/observatory/src/observatory_service/core/lifespan.py (line 38) to catch specific exception types instead of all exceptions.

Current code (lines 33-42):
    try:
        db = await aiosqlite.connect(db_uri, uri=True)
        db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row
        state.db = db
        logger.info(...)
    except Exception:
        logger.warning('Database not available at startup', extra={...})

Fix: Change to:
    except (OSError, aiosqlite.Error) as exc:
        logger.warning('Database not available at startup', extra={'path': settings.database.path, 'error': str(exc)})

This preserves graceful degradation (observatory works without DB) while letting programming errors (TypeError, AttributeError, etc.) crash startup as they should.

Verify: cd services/observatory && uv run semgrep --config ../../config/semgrep/no-silent-exception.yml --error src should report 0 findings after fix.
close_reason: Semgrep rules created and all violations fixed in commit d08a8e1

All @tool decorated, all async, all use JWS signing where required.
close_reason: Duplicate of kg1, implemented all TaskBoardMixin methods

title: [observatory] Consolidate duplicated _scalar/_fetchone/_fetchall into database.py
description:
## Problem

The Observatory service has identical copies of `_scalar()`, `_fetchone()`, and `_fetchall()` helper functions copy-pasted across four service modules:

- `services/metrics.py:24-30`
- `services/agents.py:14-20`
- `services/tasks.py:13-19`
- `services/quarterly.py:32-38`

Meanwhile, `services/database.py` already provides `execute_query` and `execute_query_one` but most modules don't use them.

A bug fix to query behavior must be applied in four places. This is a DRY HIGH violation.

## Suggested Fix

Add `scalar()`, `fetchone()`, `fetchall()` to `services/database.py`. Update all service modules to import from one place. Delete the ~60 lines of duplicated code.

Also extract the repeated ISO timestamp formatting pattern (`.isoformat(timespec='seconds').replace('+00:00', 'Z')`) into a shared `to_iso(dt)` utility.

## Principle

DRY — Don't Repeat Yourself

## Identified at

Git HEAD: `2c08c347` (2c08c3478a5baa2a126c6e09f52441ddececeb55)

title: Verify court service uses DailyRotatingFileHandler for logging
description: The shared logging module in service-commons was updated to use DailyRotatingFileHandler instead of TimedRotatingFileHandler with a namer hook. Log files are now named YYYY-MM-DD.log from creation instead of current.log. Once the court service implementation is complete, verify it uses setup_logging() from service-commons (which automatically picks up the new handler) and does not have any custom log file handling that bypasses it.
close_reason: Court service uses setup_logging() from service-commons which has DailyRotatingFileHandler. No custom log handling.

title: Write e2e authorization tests for task board (test_task_board_auth.py)
description:
Add a new e2e test file agents/tests/e2e/test_task_board_auth.py that validates all authorization and negative-path behavior for the Task Board service. Tests verify that unauthorized operations are rejected with correct HTTP status codes and error codes per the auth spec (docs/specifications/service-api/task-board-service-auth-specs.md).

10 test cases:
1. test_self_bid_rejected — poster bids on own task → 400 SELF_BID
2. test_duplicate_bid_rejected — same agent bids twice → 409 BID_ALREADY_EXISTS
3. test_non_poster_cannot_cancel_task — worker cancels → 403 FORBIDDEN
4. test_non_poster_cannot_accept_bid — worker accepts bid → 403 FORBIDDEN
5. test_non_poster_cannot_approve_task — worker approves submitted task → 403 FORBIDDEN
6. test_non_poster_cannot_dispute_task — worker disputes submitted task → 403 FORBIDDEN
7. test_non_worker_cannot_upload_asset — poster uploads to accepted task → 403 FORBIDDEN
8. test_non_worker_cannot_submit_deliverable — poster submits deliverable → 403 FORBIDDEN
9. test_approve_non_submitted_task_rejected — poster approves accepted task → 409 INVALID_STATUS
10. test_bidding_deadline_expiry_rejects_bid — bid after deadline → 409 INVALID_STATUS

All tests use _request_raw for explicit status code and error body assertions. Follow existing patterns in test_task_board.py (make_funded_agent, _close_agents, @pytest.mark.e2e).
close_reason: All 10 e2e authorization tests written and passing. Committed as ac8ad0e.

title: Add rotating file logging to service-commons
description: All 6 services currently log exclusively to stdout via service_commons.logging.setup_logging() (StreamHandler(sys.stdout)). There is no file-based log persistence anywhere. Add a TimedRotatingFileHandler to the shared logging library in libs/service-commons/src/service_commons/logging.py that writes daily rotating log files with YYYY-MM-DD.log naming to each service's data/logs/ folder. Key decisions needed before implementation: (1) file logging in addition to stdout or replacing it, (2) log directory convention (data/logs/ per service vs configurable path vs central folder). Each service's config.yaml logging section will need a new field (e.g. logging.file_path or logging.directory). The per-service logging.py wrappers are identical copy-paste and should not need changes if this is handled in service-commons.
close_reason: Implemented rotating file logging in service-commons with per-service config.yaml directory setting. All 5 implemented services pass CI. Court config.yaml updated for when it's built.

title: Task board returns 502 instead of meaningful error for missing bank account
description: When the task board proxies escrow operations to the central bank and the agent has no bank account, the central bank returns 404 (account not found). The task board doesn't handle this upstream error gracefully — it propagates as a raw 502 Bad Gateway. This affects both task posting (escrow lock) and task approval (escrow release). The task board should catch the upstream 404 and return a clear error like 'ACCOUNT_NOT_FOUND' with an appropriate status code (e.g. 422 or 400) explaining that the agent has no bank account. Demonstrated by test_agent_startup.py e2e tests.
close_reason: Task board now propagates 404/403/409 from central bank instead of masking them as 502 Bad Gateway. All three escrow methods (lock, release, split) handle 4xx responses explicitly.

title: Extract database abstractions from business logic in Identity, Task Board, and Court
description: Three of four services (Identity, Task Board, Court) hardcode sqlite3.connect() directly inside business logic classes (AgentRegistry, TaskManager, DisputeService). This violates DIP — business logic is coupled to a specific storage implementation, blocking unit testing without real databases and preventing future migration to PostgreSQL or other backends. For each service: (1) Define a repository protocol/interface in the service layer that describes the persistence operations needed. (2) Move SQLite-specific code into a concrete repository implementation. (3) Inject the repository via constructor in the business logic class. (4) The lifespan (composition root) creates the concrete SQLite repository and injects it. The Reputation service already demonstrates this pattern well with FeedbackStore — use it as the reference. Additionally, for Identity, the AgentRegistry also mixes cryptographic validation and JWS handling with persistence — consider extracting CryptoVerifier and JWSVerifier alongside the AgentRepository.
close_reason: All 3 store extractions completed: AgentStore (identity), DisputeStore (court), TaskStore (task-board). All CI passes.

title: Add frontend build step to observatory init/build workflow
description: The observatory frontend (React/Vite) has no build step in the service workflow. npm install and npm run build are never called, so frontend/dist/ is never produced. Need to: (1) add npm install + npm run build to 'just init' or create a new 'just build-frontend' command, (2) ensure the dist/ folder is produced before the service starts, (3) consider adding node/npm to the 'just check' tool verification.

title: E2E test: auto-approve on review deadline timeout
description: Write an e2e test where a task auto-approves because the poster did not review in time. Requires TaskBoardMixin. Test: register poster + worker → fund poster → post task with very short review deadline → worker bids → poster accepts → worker submits → wait for review deadline to pass → GET task → verify status auto-transitioned to APPROVED → verify escrow released to worker.
close_reason: E2E tests implemented and CI passes
